channel.title: Guitar Jail (Seeking Greenlight)
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Guitar Jail (Seeking Greenlight)" - A caucasian man in his late 20's/early 30's, in what appears to be a stone jail cell with no windows, trapped inside of an acoustic guitar.

item.title: JS Staff Post -- Forum Crit -- Info
item.content:encoded:
Noting that this user already has a thread for this idea here: http://scp-wiki.wikidot.com/forum/t-13973627/guitar-jail#post-4866212

Author, as stated in the Required Reading, do not create a new thread for a concept already on the forum, unless the thread has not been posted on in 14 days or longer. If you wish to make changes to your concept, please edit the original thread instead of creating a new one.

item.title: Re: Guitar Jail
item.content:encoded:
You may want to read the "must read" section of this forum and follow the instructions.

You also double posted.

item.title: Guitar Jail
item.content:encoded:
A caucasian man in his late 20's/ early 30's, in what appears to be a stone jail cell with no windows, trapped inside of a guitar.

The man wears an orange prisoner outfit and has no recollection of who he is or how he ended up there. He appears to be missing the thumb, index finger and pinky on his right hand. The man doesn't appear to require any sustenance and cannot reach out of the guitar due to an invisible barrier, although objects can pass through this barrier.

The jail cell appears to be made out of stone and concrete and can be observed through the soundhole of the guitar. There is a light bulb hanging from the centre of the room that keeps the room lit at all times and a bed attached to the wall. Any attempts to damage the cell from outside of the guitar have all failed and resulted in the man feeling great pain throughout his body.

The guitar is a typical 6 string acoustic guitar. The guitar seems to be indestructible. There is a logo and name of the non-existent brand "Slaves to the Sound" on the back of the guitar. Any movements the guitar makes do not appear to effect the jail cell or man. When playing the guitar, the man instinctively and perfectly mimics the playing of the guitar. The sounds produced by the guitar do not possess any anomalous properties.
